commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=10e6edaccb21e715ca835dfe0b46f538e89cb373)
commands.review_summary:
The PR broadens `movePathWithCopyFallback` to copy fallback on `EPERM` and `EEXIST` rename errors and adds mocked regression tests for those cases.

Reproducibility: yes. for the original Windows `EPERM` failure path from current-main source and regression tests. No high-confidence reproduction is available for the PR's extra `EEXIST` fallback claim or for accepting POSIX `EPERM`.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Maintainers should decide whether the remaining `EEXIST` fallback is desired; automation should not choose that API behavior while real proof is missing.

target.title: fix: fall back to copy on EPERM/EEXIST rename errors
target.body:
## Problem

`movePathWithCopyFallback` only catches `EXDEV` (cross-device) errors from `guardedRename`. On Windows, files locked by antivirus, other processes, or permission issues throw `EPERM`/`EEXIST`, causing install and update flows to fail unnecessarily.

## Root Cause

The `renameWithRetry` helper already has `copyFallbackOnPermissionError` support that handles EPERM/EEXIST, but `movePathWithCopyFallback` calls `guardedRename` directly, bypassing that logic.

## Fix

Catch `EPERM` and `EEXIST` alongside `EXDEV` in `movePathWithCopyFallback` so the copy fallback path handles Windows permission/lock errors.

## Tests

Added two new test cases:
- `falls back to copy on EPERM (Windows file lock)`
- `falls back to copy on EEXIST (destination locked)`

Both pass with `vitest run`.

## Context

This is the same issue that the local EPERM patch addressed in OpenClaw. The fix belongs in `@openclaw/fs-safe` so all consumers benefit.
target.author: liuxingwei0601
